title: Domain
short: The set of all input values for which a function is defined.
full:
The domain of a function is every x-value you are allowed to put in. Most expressions accept all real numbers, but two situations carve out exclusions: you cannot divide by zero, and you cannot take an even root (or a logarithm) of a negative number.

So the domain of 1/(x − 2) is every real number except x = 2, and the domain of √(x) is x ≥ 0. The matching set of outputs is called the range.
